en: Common mistakes in English.
One, subject-verb agreement.
She go to school every day.
She goes to school every day.
The verb must agree with the subject in number,
singular, plural.
Remember, add S, S to the verb when the subject is singular.
He, she, it.
Two, wrong tenses.
Yesterday, I go to the market.
Yesterday, I went to the market.
Learners often mix past, present, and future tenses.
Always match the verb tense with the time expression
yesterday, past tense.
Three, confusing me and I.
Me and John are friends.
John and I are friends.
Use I for subjects, me for objects.
Four, double negatives.
I don't know nothing about it.
I don't know anything about it.
Only one negative word should be used.
